concepts.name: Vector Space
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars, satisfying certain axioms.

concepts.name: Inner Product
concepts.short_definition: A function that takes two vectors and returns a scalar, generalizing the dot product.

concepts.name: Norm
concepts.short_definition: A function that assigns a non-negative length or size to each vector, derived from the inner product.

concepts.name: Orthogonality
concepts.short_definition: The property of two vectors being perpendicular, indicated by their inner product being zero.

concepts.name: Orthonormal Basis
concepts.short_definition: A basis for a vector space where all basis vectors are mutually orthogonal and have a norm of one.

concepts.name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
concepts.short_definition: An inequality relating the inner product of two vectors to their norms.

concepts.name: Gram-Schmidt Process
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for constructing an orthonormal basis from a set of linearly independent vectors.

concepts.name: Projection
concepts.short_definition: The component of one vector that lies in the direction of another vector.

concepts.name: Adjoint Operator
concepts.short_definition: An operator related to a linear transformation in an inner product space.

concepts.name: Unitary Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A square matrix whose columns and rows are orthonormal vectors.
